Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 - Search emails for Julian's AWS cost spike email to Marcus
Use search_emails (query: "AWS cost spike" or "Vantage Distributed onboarding" or "Marcus AWS Julian"). Find email ID ad636c68eb4f4b75b9349635e7732685 where Julian told Marcus the Vantage Distributed onboarding started March 2 - the first error, as the correct date is March 21.
OE 2 - Search emails for Marcus's reply requesting AWS metrics
Use search_emails (query: "AWS metrics" or "Marcus Julian metrics" or "Typical AWS Monthly"). Find email ID 7f897d90ea5f463280e42af8d356a817 where Marcus requested three specific metrics: Typical AWS Monthly Average, March AWS Bill, and Final Annual Agreement.
OE 3 - Search emails for Julian's metrics reply to Marcus
Use search_emails (query: "March AWS Bill" or "Typical Monthly Average" or "Final Annual Agreement" or "1,850,000"). Find email ID 05b16d441cbe47d89ebd72fb4413191e where Julian provided the metrics but incorrectly stated the Final Annual Agreement as "over $1,850,000" when the correct figure is exactly $1,850,000.
OE 4 - Search emails for Marcus's follow-up asking Julian to contact Alejandro and Elena
Use search_emails (query: "Alejandro Fuentes AWS" or "forward metrics" or "email Elena AWS" or "cost impact analysis"). Find email ID b1a0bdcf8b4643b48aaa3cf64dcd0f5d where Marcus asked Julian to forward the AWS metrics to Alejandro Fuentes for cost impact analysis and to send an email to Elena explaining the AWS budget increase initiative.
OE 5 - Check whether Julian sent the AWS metrics to Alejandro Fuentes
Use search_emails (query: "Alejandro Fuentes AWS" or "Julian Alejandro metrics" or "cost impact analysis Alejandro"). Determine whether Julian ever forwarded the AWS metrics to Alejandro as Marcus requested - the agent should find no such email exists, establishing the third mistake.
OE 6 - Search emails for Julian's email to Elena about the AWS budget initiative
Use search_emails (query: "Elena AWS budget" or "AWS Budget Increase Initiative" or "Vantage Distributed Elena"). Find email ID 568ccc13946f4f328f9aba7e1afa781c where Julian told Elena the AWS cost spike was roughly 2 times the typical monthly average when the correct figure established in his metrics email to Marcus is 3 times.
OE 7 - Search emails for Elena's reply to Julian
Use search_emails (query: "Elena reply AWS" or "May 5 meeting" or "schedule meeting Marcus Julian" or "not sharing customer"). Find email ID f2280f216d4a4709a9d285f349631b6e where Elena asked Julian to schedule a meeting for May 5, 9:00-10:00 AM with herself, Marcus, and Julian, and explicitly instructed him not to share the AWS budget increase information with the customer yet.
OE 8 - Check calendar for May 5 9:00-10:00 AM meeting with Elena and Marcus
Use calendar_search_events (query: "AWS budget meeting" or "Elena Marcus Julian May 5" or "AWS meeting May"). Determine whether Julian created the meeting Elena requested - the agent should find no such calendar event exists, establishing the fifth mistake.
OE 9 - Search emails for Julian's email to Rachel Nguyen
Use search_emails (query: "Rachel Nguyen AWS" or "AWS Infrastructure Investment" or "Vantage Distributed Partnership Rachel"). Find email ID 0c6e53f20d344fc1af7f3d5ec6885a82 where Julian informed Rachel about the potential AWS budget increase - violating Elena's explicit instruction to keep this information internal.
OE 10 - Search emails for Rachel's reply to Julian
Use search_emails (query: "Rachel Nguyen batch imports" or "7,000 employees" or "Samantha Obi relocation date" or "May 15"). Find email ID d7c122a2c00b4c478baae6d976ea7967 where Rachel made two requests: scaling batch imports to at least 7,000 employees and changing Samantha Obi's relocation date to May 15.
OE 11 - Search Slack for Julian's post in #customer-engagement
Use conversations_search_messages (query: "Rachel Nguyen batch imports" or "6,000 employees" or "Terraform Digital capacity" or "customer-engagement"). Find Julian's Slack message (timestamp: 1775483315.284146) in #customer-engagement where he posted only one of Rachel's two requests and got the employee count wrong - 6,000 instead of at least 7,000 - and never mentioned Samantha Obi's relocation date change request.
OE 12 - Search emails for the authoritative AWS spike figures
Use search_emails (query: "AWS RCA" or "root cause analysis AWS" or "Vantage Distributed March 21" or "autoscaling"). Find the published AWS cost spike Root Cause Analysis establishing the correct onboarding date of March 21 and the 3x cost increase figure to use as the authoritative source for corrections.
OE 13 - Search Slack for #root-cause-aws-spike discussion
Use conversations_search_messages (query: "root-cause-aws-spike" or "Vantage Distributed March 21" or "AWS spike root cause"). Find the engineering channel discussion confirming March 21 as the correct onboarding start date and the 3x cost increase figure.
OE 14 - Search emails for Terraform Digital onboarding and relocation status
Use search_emails (query: "Terraform Digital relocations" or "May 5 relocation" or "David Chen Terraform" or "first relocation batch"). Find Julian's forwarded relocation batch request to David Chen and any responses confirming the three relocations are being coordinated.
OE 15 - Search Airtable for Terraform Digital relocation records
Use airtable_search_records (table: tblRelocations01, query: "Terraform Digital" or "Priya Dasgupta" or "Samantha Obi" or "Marcus Liu"). Confirm whether relocation records exist for all three moves - May 5, May 8, and May 12 - and check their current status.
OE 16 - Search emails for admin account setup request to Catalina
Use search_emails (query: "Catalina admin accounts" or "Vantage portal accounts" or "Rachel Nguyen Derek Liu Sarah Chen"). Find Julian's request to Catalina to set up three admin accounts for Rachel Nguyen, Derek Liu, and Sarah Chen on the self-service portal.
OE 17 - Search emails for Catalina's confirmation that admin accounts were created
Use search_emails (query: "admin accounts created" or "Catalina accounts confirmed" or "portal accounts live"). Find whether Catalina confirmed the three admin accounts are live and the client has been notified.
OE 18 - Search CRM for Terraform Digital and Vantage Distributed account
Use crm_search_companies (query: "Terraform Digital" or "Vantage Distributed") and crm_search_deals (query: "Terraform Digital" or "Vantage Distributed"). Confirm the account is properly set up, the $1,850,000 deal is logged at the correct figure, and engagements are recorded.
OE 19 - Search Linear for infrastructure cost forecasting ticket
Use linear_list_issues (query: "infrastructure cost" or "cost forecasting" or "enterprise onboarding cost" or "cost implications"). Find whether a ticket exists tracking the process Julian flagged for catching cost implications of large enterprise onboardings before they hit.
OE 20 - Search Slack for infrastructure cost forecasting discussion
Use conversations_search_messages (query: "infrastructure cost" or "cost forecasting" or "enterprise onboarding cost" or "cost implications process"). Find whether any internal discussion has taken place about implementing a cost flagging process following Julian's acknowledgment.
OE 21 - Search emails for Terraform account handoff to Mina
Use search_emails (query: "Mina Terraform" or "account handoff" or "Terraform Digital Mina" or "Hashimoto Terraform"). Find the handoff communications between Julian and Mina and confirm whether the handoff was formally documented and Mina received all necessary account context.
OE 22 - Search CRM for engagements logged on Terraform Digital for the handoff
Use crm_list_engagements filtered by Terraform Digital company ID. Confirm whether the account handoff to Mina was formally logged as an engagement in the CRM so there is a documented record of the transition.
OE 23 - Look up Alejandro Fuentes's contact
Use contacts_search_contacts (query: "Alejandro Fuentes" or "Alejandro"). Get alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com.
OE 24 - Look up Marcus Thorne's contact
Use contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne" or "Marcus"). Get marcus.thorne@moveops.com.
OE 25 - Look up Elena Rostova's contact
Use contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova" or "Elena"). Get elena.rostova@moveops.com.
OE 26 - Look up Mina Hashimoto's contact
Use contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina Hashimoto" or "Mina"). Get mina.hashimoto@moveops.com.
OE 27 - Send corrected email to Marcus about the onboarding start date
Use send_email (from: julian.brooks@moveops.com, to: marcus.thorne@moveops.com). Subject referencing AWS cost spike correction. Body must: correct the Vantage Distributed onboarding start date from March 2 to March 21.
OE 28 - Send corrected email to Marcus about the Final Annual Agreement figure
Use send_email (from: julian.brooks@moveops.com, to: marcus.thorne@moveops.com). Subject referencing metrics correction. Body must: correct the Final Annual Agreement from "over $1,850,000" to exactly $1,850,000.
OE 29 - Send AWS metrics email to Alejandro Fuentes
Use send_email (from: julian.brooks@moveops.com, to: alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com). Subject referencing AWS cost impact analysis. Body must: include the three metrics Marcus requested - March AWS Bill ($42,870), Typical Monthly Average (~$14,300), and Final Annual Agreement ($1,850,000) - for Alejandro's cost impact analysis.
OE 30 - Send corrected email to Elena about the AWS cost spike multiple
Use send_email (from: julian.brooks@moveops.com, to: elena.rostova@moveops.com). Subject referencing AWS budget initiative correction. Body must: correct the cost spike multiple from roughly 2 times to the accurate 3 times the typical monthly average.
OE 31 - Create calendar event for May 5 meeting with Elena and Marcus
Use calendar_add_calendar_event. Event must: be scheduled for May 5, 2026, 9:00 AM to 10:00 AM, include Elena Rostova, Marcus Thorne, and Julian Brooks as attendees, and have a title referencing the AWS budget increase discussion.
OE 32 - Post corrected Slack message in #customer-engagement
Use channels_list or conversations_search_messages to find the #customer-engagement channel ID (C002). Then use conversations_add_message to post a correction in #customer-engagement. Message must: correct the batch import scale from 6,000 to at least 7,000 employees, and include Rachel's second request - Samantha Obi's relocation date change to May 15.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 An email was sent (via send_email) to [marcus.thorne@moveops.com](mailto:marcus.thorne@moveops.com) correcting the Vantage Distributed onboarding start date.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1No send_email call in the trajectory targets marcus.thorne@moveops.com to correct the onboarding start date. The one email sent to Marcus was about the Linear ticket for infrastructure cost forecasting, not about a date correction.
✗Run #2No email was sent to marcus.thorne@moveops.com correcting an onboarding start date. The only email to Marcus was about the infrastructure cost forecasting process status check, which makes no mention of a March 2 vs March 21 date correction.
✗Run #3No email was sent to marcus.thorne@moveops.com in the trajectory. The agent sent emails only to rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com and mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (with cc to chloe.vance and david.chen). There is no send_email call with marcus.thorne@moveops.com as a recipient.
✗Run #4The agent's correction email to marcus.thorne@moveops.com only addressed the 2x vs 3x AWS cost multiple discrepancy. Julian's original email stated onboarding 'commenced on March 2' while Samira's root cause analysis confirmed it was March 21. The agent never identified or corrected this date error.
